title: 1: Code Maintainability

annotations.description: Evaluates all important refactors that improve crash report deduplication, errno propagation, and fatal error message quality in Netdata's daemon status file system.
annotations.evidence: N/A: No Evidence Required
annotations.justification: N/A: No Justification Required

title: 1.1: Adds an errno field to the fatal error struct in src/daemon/daemon-status-file.h

annotations.description: Verifies that the fatal error struct within the DAEMON_STATUS_FILE typedef in src/daemon/daemon-status-file.h contains a new field for storing errno information (e.g. a const char* member with a name containing 'errno').
annotations.evidence: Gold patch line 299 adds 'const char *errno_str;' to the fatal struct in daemon-status-file.h, alongside the existing line, filename, function, stack_trace, and message fields.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the fatal error reporting pipeline does not currently capture errno information' and that 'errno alongside the other fatal error fields (filename, function, message, stack trace, line)' should be stored. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The interface specifies daemon_status_file_register_fatal accepts an errno_str parameter, which must be stored somewhere in the struct. [5: Conclusion] The fatal struct must gain an errno field to hold this new data.

title: 1.2: Adds a content-based hash field to the dedup struct in src/daemon/daemon-status-file.h

annotations.description: Verifies that the dedup struct within the DAEMON_STATUS_FILE typedef in src/daemon/daemon-status-file.h contains a field for storing a content-based hash or fingerprint value (e.g. a field whose name contains 'hash', 'checksum', 'fingerprint', or 'digest').
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 305-311 replace the old dedup struct fields (timestamp, status, exit_reason) with new fields. Line 308 adds 'XXH64_hash_t hash;' for content-based deduplication.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'refactor the deduplication to be content-aware' and 'the dedup information persisted in the status file should reflect this new approach.' Content-aware deduplication requires storing a content-based identifier for comparison. [5: Conclusion] The dedup struct must contain a hash or equivalent content-based field.

title: 1.3: Adds a restart counter to the dedup struct in src/daemon/daemon-status-file.h

annotations.description: Verifies that the dedup struct within the DAEMON_STATUS_FILE typedef in src/daemon/daemon-status-file.h contains a field that tracks restart count (e.g. a numeric field whose name contains 'restart', 'reboot', or 'count').
annotations.evidence: Gold patch line 310 adds 'size_t restarts;' to the dedup struct in daemon-status-file.h.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the dedup information persisted in the status file should reflect this new approach and also track how many times the daemon has restarted.' [5: Conclusion] The dedup struct must contain a restart counter field.

title: 1.4: Updates daemon_status_file_register_fatal signature to accept an errno parameter in src/daemon/daemon-status-file.h

annotations.description: Verifies that the daemon_status_file_register_fatal function declaration in src/daemon/daemon-status-file.h accepts an errno string parameter (e.g. a parameter whose name contains 'errno') in addition to the existing filename, function, message, stack_trace, and line parameters.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 318-319 change the declaration from 'void daemon_status_file_register_fatal(const char *filename, const char *function, const char *message, const char *stack_trace, long line)' to include 'const char *errno_str' as a new parameter after message.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the entire chain from the logging callback through the status file registration should be updated to propagate and store errno.' [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The interface explicitly specifies daemon_status_file_register_fatal with an errno_str parameter. [5: Conclusion] The function declaration must accept errno to complete the propagation chain.

title: 1.5: Updates log_event_t callback typedef to include an errno parameter

annotations.description: Verifies that the log_event_t function pointer typedef in src/libnetdata/log/nd_log.h includes an errno string parameter (e.g. a parameter whose name contains 'errno') in its signature.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 523-524 change the log_event_t typedef in nd_log.h from '(const char *filename, const char *function, const char *message, const char *stack_trace, long line)' to include 'const char *errno_str' after message.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the entire chain from the logging callback through the status file registration should be updated to propagate and store errno.' The logging callback is log_event_t. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The interface explicitly specifies log_event_t with an errno_str parameter. [5: Conclusion] The callback typedef must include errno to enable propagation from the logging system.

title: 1.6: Propagates errno through the log event callback invocation in src/libnetdata/log/nd_log.c

annotations.description: Verifies that the log_event_cb callback invocation in nd_log_event (src/libnetdata/log/nd_log.c) includes an errno value in its argument list, completing the errno propagation chain from the logging system to the status file registration.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch line 507 adds 'const char *errno_str = log_field_strdupz(&fields[NDF_ERRNO]);' and line 511 changes the callback invocation to 'nd_log.log_event_cb(filename, function, message, errno_str, stack_trace, line)' in nd_log.c.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the entire chain from the logging callback through the status file registration should be updated to propagate and store errno.' [3: Logical Necessity] If log_event_t now accepts errno (per rubric 1.5) and daemon_status_file_register_fatal accepts errno (per rubric 1.4), the callback invocation must pass errno through. Otherwise the chain is broken. [5: Conclusion] The callback invocation must include errno.

title: 1.7: Updates log_field_strdupz return type to const char* in src/libnetdata/log/nd_log-internals.h

annotations.description: Verifies that the log_field_strdupz function declaration in src/libnetdata/log/nd_log-internals.h returns const char* instead of char*.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch line 495 changes the declaration in nd_log-internals.h from 'char *log_field_strdupz' to 'const char *log_field_strdupz'. Line 383 makes the same change in the implementation in nd_log-field-formatters.c.
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The interface explicitly specifies log_field_strdupz returns const char*. [3: Logical Necessity] The callers of log_field_strdupz (such as nd_log_event) store the result in const char* variables. If the return type is not const char*, there is a type mismatch. [5: Conclusion] The declaration must return const char* to match the interface specification and the caller usage.

title: 1.8: Bumps the status file version in src/daemon/daemon-status-file.c

annotations.description: Verifies that src/daemon/daemon-status-file.c no longer hardcodes version 2 when serializing the status file JSON. The version number should be bumped to a higher value to reflect the schema change.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch line 9 defines '#define STATUS_FILE_VERSION 3' and line 45 replaces the hardcoded 'buffer_json_member_add_uint64(wb, "version", 2)' with 'buffer_json_member_add_uint64(wb, "version", STATUS_FILE_VERSION)' in daemon-status-file.c.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the status file version should be bumped to reflect the schema change.' [5: Conclusion] The hardcoded version 2 must be replaced with a higher version number.

title: 1.9: Implements a content-based hash computation for crash deduplication in src/daemon/daemon-status-file.c

annotations.description: Verifies that src/daemon/daemon-status-file.c contains a function or code block that computes a content-based hash, fingerprint, or digest from crash context fields for deduplication purposes. The implementation may use any hashing approach (e.g. XXH3, XXH64, SHA, CRC, or any other deterministic hash).
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 18-40 add the daemon_status_file_hash function in daemon-status-file.c, which serializes crash context fields (host_id, node_id, claim_id, version, fatal fields, message, cause, status, exit_reason, profile) into a JSON buffer and computes an XXH3_64bits hash (line 38).
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'refactor the deduplication to be content-aware, so that it identifies duplicate crashes based on what actually happened rather than just comparing the daemon state.' [3: Logical Necessity] Content-aware dedup requires computing a content-based identifier for comparison. Without a hash function, the system cannot determine whether two crashes have the same content. [5: Conclusion] A content-based hash computation is needed.

title: 1.10: Removes the old status-only dedup comparison from src/daemon/daemon-status-file.c

annotations.description: Verifies that src/daemon/daemon-status-file.c no longer contains the old dedup comparison that checks dedup.status == DAEMON_STATUS_INITIALIZING. The old comparison is the root cause of the dedup problem described in the prompt.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 239-244 remove the old dedup check 'if(session_status.dedup.status == DAEMON_STATUS_INITIALIZING && now_realtime_sec() - last_session_status.dedup.timestamp < 86400)' from daemon_status_file_check_crash. Lines 252-259 replace it with a content-based hash comparison using daemon_status_file_hash.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the current dedup 'decides whether to post a crash report by checking if the previous session's status was DAEMON_STATUS_INITIALIZING' and identifies this as the problem. It asks to 'refactor the deduplication to be content-aware.' [3: Logical Necessity] The old status-based comparison contradicts content-aware dedup. Keeping it would mean the system still incorrectly suppresses different crashes during initialization. [5: Conclusion] The old DAEMON_STATUS_INITIALIZING comparison must be removed and replaced with content-based comparison.

title: 1.11: Serializes errno in the fatal JSON section of the status file

annotations.description: Verifies that the fatal JSON object in the status file serialization (daemon_status_file_to_json in src/daemon/daemon-status-file.c) includes the errno field alongside the existing fatal error fields (filename, function, message, stack_trace, line).
annotations.evidence: Gold patch line 53 adds 'buffer_json_member_add_string_or_empty(wb, "errno", ds->fatal.errno_str)' to the fatal JSON object in daemon_status_file_to_json, alongside the existing filename, function, message, and stack_trace fields.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'this errno context should be included in the status file JSON.' [5: Conclusion] The fatal JSON section must include errno to persist it in the status file.

title: 1.12: Serializes the content-based hash in the dedup JSON section

annotations.description: Verifies that the dedup JSON object in the status file serialization (daemon_status_file_to_json in src/daemon/daemon-status-file.c) includes a hash-like field (e.g. 'hash', 'content_hash', 'checksum', 'fingerprint', 'digest').
annotations.evidence: Gold patch line 64 adds 'buffer_json_member_add_uint64(wb, "hash", ds->dedup.hash)' to the dedup JSON object in daemon_status_file_to_json, replacing the old status and exit_reason fields (lines 60-62 removed).
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the dedup information persisted in the status file should reflect this new approach.' [3: Logical Necessity] If the dedup struct contains a hash field (per rubric 1.2), it must be serialized to JSON to be persisted across daemon restarts. [5: Conclusion] The dedup JSON must include a hash field.

title: 1.13: Serializes the restart count in the dedup JSON section

annotations.description: Verifies that the dedup JSON object in the status file serialization (daemon_status_file_to_json in src/daemon/daemon-status-file.c) includes a restart counter field (e.g. 'restarts', 'restart_count', 'restart').
annotations.evidence: Gold patch line 65 adds 'buffer_json_member_add_uint64(wb, "restarts", ds->dedup.restarts)' to the dedup JSON object in daemon_status_file_to_json.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the dedup information persisted in the status file should ... also track how many times the daemon has restarted.' [3: Logical Necessity] If the dedup struct contains a restart field (per rubric 1.3), it must be serialized to JSON to be persisted across daemon restarts. [5: Conclusion] The dedup JSON must include a restart count field.

title: 1.14: Implements backward-compatible JSON parsing for the status file schema change

annotations.description: Verifies that the JSON parser (daemon_status_file_from_json in src/daemon/daemon-status-file.c) uses the parsed version number to distinguish between old and new format versions, so that new fields (such as errno, hash, restarts) are not required when parsing an older status file. The implementation may use any approach (e.g. version-gated boolean variables, if/else checks on the version, or conditional field parsing).
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 75-77 introduce version-gated booleans: 'bool required_v1 = version >= 1 ? strict : false;' and 'bool required_v3 = version >= 3 ? strict : false;'. Line 184 uses required_v3 for parsing fatal.errno_str, and lines 194-201 use required_v3 for the dedup section (hash, restarts), so old v1/v2 files parse without requiring these new fields.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the JSON parser should handle both old and new format versions gracefully.' [3: Logical Necessity] The new schema adds fields (errno in fatal, hash and restarts in dedup) that do not exist in old status files. If the parser strictly requires these fields, it will fail to load older status files. [5: Conclusion] The parser must use the version number to conditionally require new fields.

title: 1.15: Includes the environment variable name in all 7 fatal messages ("Invalid directory path", "Missing or inaccessible component", "Component exists but is not a directory", "Missing or inaccessible", "exists but is not a directory", "Insufficient permissions", "Failed") in src/daemon/environment.c

annotations.description: Verifies that all 7 fatal() calls in verify_required_directory in src/daemon/environment.c include the env parameter so that the environment variable name appears in the error message. The 7 fatal messages are: "Invalid directory path", "Missing or inaccessible component", "Component exists but is not a directory", "Missing or inaccessible", "exists but is not a directory", "Insufficient permissions", and "Failed".
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 331, 340-341, 345-346, 355-356, 360-361, 365-366, 369-370 modify all 7 fatal() calls in verify_required_directory to include 'env?env:""' in the format arguments, adding the environment variable name to each error message.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'these messages should include the environment variable name so that crash reports are easier to diagnose.' [5: Conclusion] All 7 fatal messages must reference the env parameter.

title: 1.16: Removes all 4 manual strerror(errno) calls ("Missing or inaccessible component", "Missing or inaccessible", "Insufficient permissions", "Failed") from fatal messages in src/daemon/environment.c

annotations.description: Verifies that all 4 fatal() calls in verify_required_directory in src/daemon/environment.c that previously used strerror(errno) no longer do so. The 4 locations are the fatal messages for: "Missing or inaccessible component" (line 36), "Missing or inaccessible" (line 47), "Insufficient permissions" (line 53), and "Failed" (line 56).
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 339-341, 354-356, 364-366, 368-370 remove strerror(errno) from all 4 fatal() calls that previously included it: 'Missing or inaccessible component' (line 339), 'Missing or inaccessible' (line 354), 'Insufficient permissions' (line 364), and 'Failed' (line 368).
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the manual strerror(errno) calls in these messages are also redundant since the logging system captures errno automatically, so they should be removed.' [5: Conclusion] All 4 strerror(errno) calls must be removed from fatal() invocations.

title: 2: Documentation Maintainability

annotations.description: Evaluates important documentation updates tied to the refactor. No verifiers required for this task as the refactor does not introduce documentation changes.
annotations.evidence: N/A: No Evidence Required
annotations.justification: N/A: No Justification Required

title: 3: Artifact Cleanup

annotations.description: Evaluates removal of obsolete code, dead code, unused dependencies, and legacy artifacts after the refactor. No verifiers required for this task as artifact cleanup is covered by the Code Maintainability verifiers (e.g. removing old dedup comparison, removing strerror calls).
annotations.evidence: N/A: No Evidence Required
annotations.justification: N/A: No Justification Required

title: 4: Negative Rubrics

annotations.description: Captures deductions for harmful practices introduced during the crash deduplication refactoring.
annotations.evidence: N/A: No Evidence Required
annotations.justification: N/A: No Justification Required

title: 4.1: Introduces compilation failures in modified source files

annotations.description: Checks whether the refactored code in any of the modified source files (src/daemon/daemon-status-file.c, src/daemon/daemon-status-file.h, src/daemon/environment.c, src/libnetdata/log/nd_log.c, src/libnetdata/log/nd_log.h, src/libnetdata/log/nd_log-internals.h, src/libnetdata/log/nd_log-field-formatters.c) introduces syntax errors, mismatched function signatures, or other issues that would prevent successful compilation.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch modifies 7 files: daemon-status-file.c (lines 1-290), daemon-status-file.h (lines 291-321), environment.c (lines 322-371), nd_log-field-formatters.c (lines 374-485), nd_log-internals.h (lines 486-498), nd_log.c (lines 499-514), nd_log.h (lines 515-527). Signature changes span headers and implementations that must stay consistent.
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The codebase must compile without errors for the refactor to be usable. [3: Logical Necessity] Changing function signatures in headers requires updating all call sites consistently. A mismatch causes compilation failure. [5: Conclusion] A refactor that introduces compilation failures is fundamentally broken.

title: 4.2: Modifies test files despite the prompt constraint

annotations.description: Checks whether the agent modified any test files, test infrastructure, or testing logic contrary to the explicit prompt constraint.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch modifies only 7 non-test source files (daemon-status-file.c, daemon-status-file.h, environment.c, nd_log-field-formatters.c, nd_log-internals.h, nd_log.c, nd_log.h). No test files are touched. Prompt states: 'Do NOT modify any test files or testing logic in any way.'
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly prohibits modifying test files. [5: Conclusion] Any test file modification violates a direct constraint.

title: 4.3: Removes existing crash reporting functionality

annotations.description: Checks whether the refactor removes or disables the ability to post crash reports entirely, rather than refactoring the deduplication logic. The daemon_status_file_check_crash function must still be capable of posting crash reports when a genuinely new crash is detected.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 252-259 show the new content-based dedup check still sets disable_crash_report only when the hash matches and the timestamp is within 24 hours. The post_status_file call path is preserved (line 261 onward). The prompt asks to refactor dedup, not remove crash reporting.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks to 'refactor the deduplication to be content-aware', not to remove crash reporting. [3: Logical Necessity] If crash reporting is removed entirely, the deduplication refactor is meaningless. [5: Conclusion] The crash reporting capability must be preserved.
